cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at the news text and the question. The question asks which of the options is wrong according to the news. First, let me go through each option and compare it with the information in the text. Option A: "will give up the space program because of the accident." Looking at the text, the last paragraph mentions President Bush saying, "America's space program will go on." This directly contradicts option A, which claims that the space program will be given up. So A is likely the wrong statement. Option B: "NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster." The text says, "NASA launched an investigation into the disaster and began searching for the astronauts' remains. It said that although there had been some data failures it was too early to nail down a precise cause." This supports option B, so B is correct according to the text. Option C: "Before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded in the course of landing." The text states, "Take-off and re-entry into Earth's atmosphere are the most dangerous parts of a space mission. In 42 years of US' human space flight, there had never been an accident in the descent to Earth or landing. Challenger exploded just after take-off." This means before Columbia, no shuttles had exploded during landing (descent), so C is correct according to the text.
